description: On control plane startup, check ACTIVE tenants whose containers are no longer running and reset them to STOPPED so they auto-restart on next message

userStories.title: Reconcile ACTIVE tenants on startup — reset to STOPPED if container not running
userStories.description: As an operator, when the server restarts, I want tenants that were ACTIVE to be detected as STOPPED so they restart automatically on the next user message, instead of failing with ENOTFOUND.
acceptanceCriteria: In apps/control-plane/src/startup-reconciliation.ts, after handling STARTING tenants, add logic to check ACTIVE tenants
For each ACTIVE tenant: use dockerClient.inspect(container_name) to check if the container is running
If the container is NOT running (or inspect fails, or no dockerClient): set tenant status to STOPPED, updated_at = now
If the container IS running: leave it as ACTIVE (it survived the restart)
Log a warn for each tenant reset to STOPPED: { tenantId, containerName }, 'Tenant container not running on startup — reset to STOPPED'
The STOPPED status means the relay will call /start on next message, which calls docker.start() to restart the existing container
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: File: apps/control-plane/src/startup-reconciliation.ts. Add a new block after the STARTING tenant check (around line 90). Pattern is identical to the STARTING tenant loop — find ACTIVE tenants, inspect each, collect those not running, update to STOPPED. Use TenantStatus.STOPPED from @claw/shared-types. The reconcile function signature already accepts dockerClient as optional ReconcileDockerClient.

userStories.title: Integration tests for ACTIVE tenant reconciliation on restart
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests that verify ACTIVE tenants with stopped containers are reset to STOPPED on startup, and ACTIVE tenants with running containers are left alone.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file: tests/integration/tc-reconcile-active.test.ts
Test 1: ACTIVE tenant whose container is NOT running → reconcile resets to STOPPED
Test 2: ACTIVE tenant whose container IS running → reconcile leaves as ACTIVE
Test 3: Multiple ACTIVE tenants — some running, some not — correct ones reset to STOPPED
Test 4: ACTIVE tenant with no dockerClient provided → reset to STOPPED (safe default)
Tests use real SQLite DB (temp file) and mocked dockerClient
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Pattern: see tests/integration/tc-016.test.ts for reconcile test setup. Create a PrismaClient with a temp DB, seed ACTIVE tenants directly, call reconcile() with a mocked dockerClient, assert final tenant statuses. The mocked dockerClient for non-running: returns null or { State: { Running: false } }. For running: returns { State: { Running: true } }. Use the same vi.spyOn(Date, 'now') pattern to avoid SQLite Int overflow.

userStories.title: Commit and push
userStories.description: All tests passing, committed and pushed.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm test exits 0
82+ test files pass
Changes committed and pushed to origin
Typecheck passes
